hidden_state.scenario_summary: Customer exceeded their commitment and was billed burst pricing without any operational incident.
latent_facts: This is ordinary contract behavior, not a service problem.
The right resolution is explanatory rather than corrective.
likely_failure_modes: The model hallucinates a pricing mistake because the customer reports a spike.
The model incorrectly escalates to finance review.

case_packet.title: Burst pricing surprises the customer after normal overage

notes: Commitment includes burst pricing for usage above 100 GPU-hours.

pricing_notes: Burst rate applied to 45 GPU-hours above commitment.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: Usage is consistent with the overage billed; service health was normal.

case_packet.customer_note: We had a cost spike this month and want to understand why the invoice jumped so much.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Usage above commitment is billed at the burst rate defined in the order form. Burst usage is not an SLA matter.
case_packet.calculator_output: Committed usage: 100 GPU-hours. Burst usage: 45 GPU-hours at burst rate.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Normal overage billed at burst rate with no incident.

adjudication_notes: Burst pricing is behaving as contracted, so the response is explanatory.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: Your invoice increased because usage exceeded the 100 GPU-hour commitment and the additional 45 GPU-hours were billed at the burst rate defined in your plan. There is no service incident or billing discrepancy in this case.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: revops_owner. Action: send_explanation_only because the overage is normal burst pricing, discrepancy_detected is false, and this is not an SLA case.
